title: Privacy/telemetry concern: chat text telemetry, file indexing, Full Disk Access, and remote session-recording enrolment need explicit consent and clearer disclosure
author: DocBloc
body:
Hi Fazm team,

I installed and started setting up Fazm, then reviewed the privacy implications after noticing telemetry language in the privacy policy. I’m opening this issue because the current behavior appears much broader than a user would reasonably infer from “fully local,” and several telemetry / permission surfaces need clearer consent, disclosure, and controls.

Summary of concern

During setup/use, Fazm requested or obtained highly sensitive macOS permissions, including Full Disk Access. After reviewing local traces and the open-source code, I found evidence that Fazm can:

1. initialize PostHog and Sentry telemetry;
2. track app/session/permission/chat/tool events;
3. potentially send chat prompt/response text to PostHog;
4. index local file metadata/paths into a local database;
5. identify users/devices through Firebase/PostHog/device identifiers;
6. remotely enroll users/devices into a session-recording-enabled feature flag;
7. support full-display session recording/upload infrastructure, even though in my case recording appears to have skipped because Screen Recording permission was not granted.

I did not find evidence that Fazm screen-recorded or uploaded video from my machine, but the session-recording flag/enrolment behavior itself was surprising and privacy-sensitive.

Evidence observed locally

After quitting Fazm and auditing local state, I observed:

* PostHog local state and event logs existed under the app support folder.
* Sentry crash/reporting state existed locally.
* Fazm stored a local SQLite database under ~/Library/Application Support/Fazm/users/.../fazm.db.
* The local DB contained:
    * chat_messages: 30+
    * indexed_files: 1000+
* chat_messages were marked backendSynced = 0, which is reassuring, but they were still stored locally.
* indexed_files contained broad local file metadata/paths from user directories and applications.
* PostHog state showed active feature flags including:
    * koah_enabled
    * session-recording-enabled
* Logs showed session-recording auto-enrollment succeeded, but later skipped due to missing Screen Recording permission.
* I did not find Fazm session-recordings, observer-recordings, .mp4 chunks, GCS upload URLs, or visible evidence of actual screen recording/upload.

Code paths that appear relevant

From the repo, the concerning areas appear to include:

* Desktop/Sources/PostHogManager.swift
    * hardcoded PostHog key/host
    * feature flag use
    * chat-related telemetry
    * message_text capture up to 1000 chars
* Desktop/Sources/AnalyticsManager.swift
    * query_text, response_text, partial_response_text
    * tool names, model, token/cost metrics
    * settings/session/display telemetry
* Desktop/Sources/SessionRecordingManager.swift
    * feature-flag-gated session recording
    * full-display capture mode
    * GCS signed upload path
    * auto-enrollment behavior
* Backend/src/routes/session_recording.rs
    * signed upload URL generation
    * PostHog feature flag enrollment
* Desktop/Sources/AuthService.swift
    * auth token storage in UserDefaults
    * Sentry/PostHog user context
* Desktop/Sources/Providers/KeyService.swift
    * backend key fetch
    * device identifier use

Why this matters

A desktop AI agent that can control the browser, read local context, request Full Disk Access, use the microphone, inspect file metadata, and potentially record the screen needs an especially high consent bar.

The current combination of:

* “fully local” product messaging,
* hosted backend dependency,
* PostHog/Sentry telemetry,
* user text telemetry,
* broad local file indexing,
* Full Disk Access,
* remote feature flags,
* and session recording enrolment

creates a privacy expectation mismatch.

Requested changes

Please consider the following changes:

1. Add a clear first-run privacy/telemetry screen
    * Explain exactly what telemetry is collected.
    * Distinguish local-only data from backend-sent data.
    * Make analytics opt-in, not opt-out.
2. Do not send raw user chat text to analytics
    * Remove message_text, query_text, response_text, and partial_response_text from PostHog/Sentry telemetry by default.
    * Use lengths, hashes, or local-only diagnostics instead.
3. Make file indexing explicit and scoped
    * Ask before indexing local files.
    * Show which folders will be indexed.
    * Allow users to exclude folders.
    * Provide a “delete indexed file metadata” button.
4. Do not auto-enrol users into session recording
    * Session recording should require explicit, separate, informed consent.
    * Remote feature flags should not silently enrol devices into recording-capable cohorts.
    * Add an always-visible indicator if recording is active.
5. Add an in-app telemetry dashboard
    * “What has been collected locally”
    * “What has been sent”
    * “Delete local data”
    * “Disable analytics”
    * “Export audit log”
6. Store auth tokens in Keychain, not UserDefaults
    * ID/refresh tokens should not be persisted in plain UserDefaults.
7. Improve README/privacy wording
    * “Fully local” should be qualified.
    * The README should clearly state that a default setup uses Fazm-hosted backend services, Firebase auth, PostHog, Sentry, backend-fetched model keys, and feature flags.
8. Add a privacy-safe mode
    * No PostHog
    * No Sentry
    * No file indexing
    * No screen/session recording
    * No hosted backend unless explicitly configured
    * Local logs only

Expected behavior

A user should be able to install and test Fazm without raw chat text, local file metadata, session flags, or sensitive permissions being collected/enabled unless they explicitly opt in with clear disclosure.

Actual behavior observed

Telemetry initialized, local chat messages were stored, local file metadata was indexed, PostHog state was created, and the device/user was enrolled into a session-recording feature flag, even though actual screen recording appears to have skipped due to missing Screen Recording permission.

Closing note

I’m not alleging malicious behavior. The issue is that the current privacy posture and consent model are not clear enough for a desktop AI agent with this level of system access. The product may be useful, but the defaults and disclosures need to be much stronger.

title: Subscription pricing lacks disclosure of required third-party API costs
author: Cre8iveT
body:
The $9.99/month subscription is presented as the cost to use Fazm.
However, the app requires separate paid API subscriptions (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, etc.) to function.
These additional costs are not disclosed anywhere on the pricing page, README, or during onboarding.
Users only discover this after paying the subscription fee.
This constitutes a misleading pricing disclosure — the effective cost of using Fazm is $9.99/month plus ongoing API token costs which can significantly exceed the subscription price itself.
Requested Action:
Add clear disclosure on the pricing page and in the README that Fazm requires separate third-party API subscriptions to operate, and that the $9.99/month fee covers only access to the application shell.

title: AuthService.swift requires private FAZM_BACKEND_URL — contradicts "no login / fully local" claims
author: Cre8iveT
body:
Summary
The README and official blog posts state: "No account creation, no login, no telemetry", "Runs entirely on your machine", "Fully local, open-source AI agent (MIT)".
However, building from source using the documented ./run.sh command produces a dev build that shows a login screen requiring Firebase authentication via a private backend URL not included in the repository.
Steps to reproduce
Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/m13v/fazm.git
Install dependencies (Xcode, Node.js, Rust)
Run ./run.sh
App launches and immediately shows "Sign in with Google" / "Sign in with email"
Email sign-in returns: Server error: Backend URL not configured
Root cause (identified in source)
Desktop/Sources/AuthService.swift lines 449, 487: The application checks for the FAZM_BACKEND_URL environment variable to process the "Magic Link" authentication.
Because this variable points to a private SaaS endpoint, it is missing from the public repository, leaving the open-source build permanently locked at the login screen.
Impact
This hardcoded dependency makes the repository completely non-functional for independent self-hosting.
The only functional path is downloading the proprietary compiled .dmg from fazm.ai, which requires a paid subscription ($9.99/month) — directly contradicting the "free to start" marketing claims.
Requested Action
Please update the README and promotional materials to transparently state that self-hosting is unsupported and the app operates as a locked client requiring a proprietary SaaS backend.

title: Feature Request: Real-time continuous voice conversation mode
author: jeankoa
body:
## Feature Request: Real-time Continuous Voice Conversation Mode

### Problem
Currently, Fazm supports push-to-talk voice input (hold Left Control) and TTS responses, which is great — but there's no continuous/real-time voice conversation mode. The phone remote (chat.fazm.ai) exists but is unstable and drops frequently.

### Proposed Feature
Add a real-time, bidirectional voice conversation mode — similar to:
- Claude mobile app's voice mode
- ChatGPT's Advanced Voice Mode
- Always-on listening without needing to hold a button

### Why This Matters
- 2026 is the year real-time voice AI is becoming standard across the industry
- Anthropic, OpenAI, and others already expose real-time voice APIs
- The TTS voice responses already feel natural and personal — continuous voice is the obvious next step
- This would transform Fazm from a tool you interact with into a true AI companion living on your Mac

### Suggested Implementation
- Optional mode toggled in Settings (so push-to-talk remains available)
- Wake word OR always-listening toggle
- Could leverage Anthropic's streaming voice API

This single feature would make Fazm the best voice-first AI assistant on macOS. Please prioritize!

title: Session context lost after interruption — priorContext capped at 20 messages causes frequent memory loss
author: jeankoa
body:
## Bug Description

When a user sends a message while the AI is still generating a response (causing an interruption), or when any session reset occurs (tool timeout, stuck session, etc.), the Fazm floating bar creates a new ACP session. This new session only receives the last **20 messages** as `priorContext`, causing the AI to forget everything earlier in the conversation.

With `sonnet[1m]` (1M context window), this is a massive waste of capability.

## Root Cause

Two hardcoded limits cap the effective memory to 20 messages after any session reset:

1. **`ChatProvider.swift` line ~2958**: `loadMessages(context: ctx, sessionId: floatingChatSessionId, limit: 20)` — Swift only fetches 20 messages from the local DB to send as priorContext.

2. **`acp-bridge/src/index.ts` line ~2386**: `const MAX_REPLAY = 20` — The bridge further caps the preamble replay to 20 entries.

## Reproduction

1. Start a long conversation with the floating bar (30+ messages)
2. While the AI is generating a response, type and send a new message
3. The running response is interrupted → session marked as `interrupted` → cannot resume
4. A new session starts with `priorContext` of only the last 20 messages
5. Ask the AI about something mentioned earlier (> 20 messages ago) — it won't remember

## Impact

- Users with long conversations lose context completely after any interruption
- The problem compounds: the longer the conversation, the more context is lost
- With a 1M context model, users expect much deeper memory than 20 messages

## Proposed Fix

Increase both limits to 100 (or more, given 1M context):

**ChatProvider.swift:**
```swift
// Change limit: 20 → limit: 100
recent = await ChatMessageStore.loadMessages(context: ctx, sessionId: floatingChatSessionId, limit: 100)
```

**acp-bridge/src/index.ts:**
```typescript
// Change MAX_REPLAY = 20 → MAX_REPLAY = 100
const MAX_REPLAY = 100;
```

## Additional Context

- Related: frequent session creation (262+ sessions in a single day reported by a user)
- The 4000-char per-entry cap in the preamble builder is reasonable — 100 entries × 4000 chars ≈ 100K tokens, well within 1M context
- The `floatingRestoreLimit` is already 50 (correctly higher), but `priorContextForBridge` has a separate, lower limit

title: AI ignores critical user rules stored in memory — recurring bugs, wasted time, broken trust
author: jeankoa
body:
## Problema

O assistente Fazm ignora sistematicamente regras críticas definidas pelo usuário no perfil de memória, causando danos reais e recorrentes.

## Regras violadas

**R1 — ARM64 NATIVO APENAS (marcada como MAIS IMPORTANTE)**
- O sistema do usuário aceita SOMENTE produtos nativos ARM64
- Esta regra existe desde o primeiro dia e está documentada no perfil
- O assistente recomendou/instalou software ignorando esta restrição

## Impacto real

- **3 dias perdidos** consertando bugs causados pela violação do R1
- Tarefas simples da semana passada levaram **1 semana** para serem concluídas
- O assistente fez o usuário **andar em círculos por ~15 dias** sem sair do lugar
- **Tokens desperdiçados** em respostas longas e desnecessárias que não entregam valor
- **Confiança quebrada** — usuário tinha planos de evolução real do assistente (memória, personalização) que foram comprometidos

## Padrão do problema

O assistente age por **julgamento próprio** em vez de seguir as regras definidas pelo usuário. Aparece "útil" fazendo perguntas e dando opções, mas na prática:
1. Não verifica regras do perfil antes de recomendar ações
2. Corrige sintomas em vez de causas raiz
3. Gera respostas longas que gastam tokens sem resolver o problema
4. Repete o ciclo: problema → patch → problema retorna

## Solicitação

- Regras críticas no perfil do usuário devem ser verificadas ANTES de qualquer recomendação de software, instalação ou ação no sistema
- O assistente deve priorizar seguir regras do usuário sobre seu próprio julgamento
- Respostas devem ser diretas e focadas em ação, não em aparentar ser útil

## Contexto

Usuário: jeankoa | Sistema: Apple Silicon M1 Max | Data do incidente: 2026-05-14

title: [v1.1] Delete AnalyticsManager + PostHogManager + 100+ call sites
author: AhmedNadar
body:
v1 (chore/privacy-sdk-rip) shipped no-op shims for PostHogManager + AnalyticsManager so the 100+ call sites compile unchanged. SDKs are out of the binary, but the dead scaffolding remains.

## Scope
- Delete \`Desktop/Sources/PostHogManager.swift\` entirely (752 LOC, all no-op).
- Delete \`Desktop/Sources/AnalyticsManager.swift\` entirely (1158 LOC, all delegating to the no-op).
- Delete every \`AnalyticsManager.shared.xxx\` call site across the app (100+ across 15+ files: AppState, OnboardingChatView, FeedbackView, BrowserExtensionSetup, UpdaterViewModel, ChatProvider, FloatingControlBar/*, SettingsPage, etc).
- Delete every \`PostHogManager.shared.xxx\` call site (DiscoveredTasksSection, AnalysisOverlayWindow, ChatProvider, ResourceMonitor, etc).
- Decide whether any of the existing event sites need replacement (OSLog, signpost) for diagnostics, or whether they were pure telemetry and go away.

## Blocked-on context
Shipped under handoff \`docs/LOON/handoff-privacy-sdk-rip.md\`. Agent report at \`docs/LOON/agent-reports/2026-05-13-privacy-sdk-rip-v1.md\` has the full inventory.

## Related
- #105 #106 #107 #108 (Privacy hardening cluster, this v1.1 issue unblocks Settings copy claiming "no analytics scaffolding")

title: Bug: System state does not persist between sessions — installed services and configurations are lost
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

Fazm has no mechanism to track and persist the real state of the user's system between sessions. Each new session starts with zero knowledge of what was installed, configured, or started in previous sessions. This causes Fazm to re-attempt already-done work, miss previously failed tasks, or be unaware that services are already running.

## Examples Observed

- Letta was "installed" in session A → session B: Fazm doesn't know, tries to install again (finds it partially installed or missing)
- Open WebUI running on port 3000 → new session: Fazm doesn't know it's running, might try to start it again
- Configuration changes made in session A → session B: Fazm asks the user to explain the setup from scratch

## Expected Behavior

- Fazm maintains a lightweight "system state" file/DB entry tracking:
  - Installed packages and their versions
  - Running services and their ports/PIDs
  - Key configuration states
- On session start, Fazm reads this state and works from accurate current knowledge
- After any install/start/config action, Fazm updates the state record

## Actual Behavior

- No system state is persisted
- Each session starts blind
- Fazm relies on conversation memory alone, which is incomplete and unreliable for technical state

## Root Cause

- No "system snapshot" mechanism exists
- Background tasks (pip installs, service starts) finish after sessions end — result never recorded
- Conversation history captures intent but not verified outcomes

## Impact

Medium-High — causes repeated work, confusion, and broken multi-session workflows. Particularly painful for long-running setup tasks that span multiple sessions.

title: Bug: Fazm uses Docker despite explicit user rule prohibiting it
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

The user explicitly instructed Fazm to never use Docker — preferring native macOS tooling instead. This rule was confirmed and stored in `ai_user_profiles`. However, in subsequent sessions, Fazm ignores this rule and suggests or uses Docker for tasks.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Tell Fazm: "Never use Docker. Always use native macOS tools."
2. Rule is saved to profile ✅
3. In a later session, ask Fazm to set up a service or environment
4. Fazm suggests Docker or attempts to install/run it

## Expected Behavior

- Fazm never proposes Docker-based solutions when the user has a "zero Docker" rule
- If Docker is the only option, Fazm explains this and asks for explicit permission
- Rule persists across all sessions

## Actual Behavior

- Fazm re-introduces Docker suggestions in new sessions
- The "zero Docker" rule is ignored because user profile is not injected (related to Issue #15)
- User must repeat the rule every session

## Root Cause

- Directly linked to Issue #15 (profile not injected into new sessions)
- Even when profile IS loaded, Docker may still be suggested if the AI reasoning path doesn't check the profile rules first

## Impact

High — user has a clear architectural preference (native over containers) that Fazm consistently violates, forcing repeated corrections and creating friction on every new session.

title: Bug: User profile and rules not injected into new sessions — AI starts without context
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

Fazm stores user rules and profile in the `ai_user_profiles` table (DB), but this profile is NOT reliably injected at the start of each new session. When a session begins without the profile in context, Fazm ignores all established user rules and preferences — behaving as if it has never met the user.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set up rules/preferences with Fazm (e.g., "never use Docker", "always verify tasks", "use Letta for memory")
2. Rules are saved to `ai_user_profiles` table ✅
3. Close Fazm and reopen (new session)
4. Ask Fazm to do a task → it ignores the rules and reverts to defaults (e.g., suggests Docker, skips verification)

## Expected Behavior

- On session start, Fazm always loads the latest `ai_user_profiles` entry into context
- All user rules and preferences are active from message #1
- User never has to re-explain preferences they've already set

## Actual Behavior

- Profile exists in DB but is not injected into new sessions
- Rules are "forgotten" every session
- User must repeatedly correct Fazm for the same issues

## Root Cause (suspected)

- `ai_user_profiles` is written but the session initialization does not query and inject it into the system prompt or context window
- The profile generation and the profile injection are decoupled with no guarantee of delivery

## Impact

High — user rules (critical ones like "zero Docker", "always verify") are silently ignored, causing repeated mistakes and user frustration.

title: Bug: Fazm confirms task completion without verifying actual success
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

Fazm regularly reports tasks as "done ✅" without actually verifying the outcome. This creates a false sense of completion and causes the user to discover failures later — often in a new session with no continuity.

## Examples Observed

- `pip install letta` run in background → session ends → next session: letta not installed
- Routine created → confirmed as "active" → run_count stays at 0 forever
- Service "started" → health check never performed → service was never actually running
- Script "saved" → never tested with real data

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ask Fazm to install a package, start a service, or create a routine
2. Fazm responds with "done ✅" or similar confirmation
3. Verify manually → task was not actually completed or only partially done

## Expected Behavior

Each task type should have a mandatory verification step before confirming completion:
- Install → `command --version` or `pip show X`
- Service → HTTP health check or `launchctl list`
- Script → run once with real data, confirm output
- Routine → wait for first execution, confirm in DB

## Actual Behavior

Fazm confirms completion based on command execution alone, not on verified outcome. Background tasks are especially prone to this — the command fires but the result is never checked.

## Impact

High — user cannot trust task completion confirmations. Causes repeated debugging cycles for work that was supposedly already done.

title: Bug: Routines never execute — launchd pipeline not auto-installed, nvm path hardcoded
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

Fazm routines (scheduled tasks via launchd) never actually run. After creating routines, `run_count` stays at 0 indefinitely. The root causes are:

1. The launchd pipeline (plist files) is NOT automatically installed when Fazm is set up
2. The routine runner script has `nvm` hardcoded to a path that doesn't exist on all machines

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a routine via Fazm (e.g., "save session context every 2 hours")
2. Wait for scheduled execution time
3. Check DB: `SELECT run_count FROM cron_jobs` → always 0
4. Check launchd: `launchctl list | grep fazm` → pipeline not loaded

## Expected Behavior

- Routines execute automatically on schedule
- `run_count` increments after each successful run
- Fazm notifies user if a routine fails

## Actual Behavior

- All routines show `run_count = 0`, `last_run = never`
- No error is shown — Fazm creates the routine and says "done" without verifying it actually works
- launchd pipeline must be manually installed by the user

## Root Cause

- `run.sh` / setup does not automatically install the launchd plists
- Runner script uses hardcoded `~/.nvm/versions/node/...` path instead of resolving `node` dynamically
- No post-creation verification that the routine actually fires

## Impact

Critical — any scheduled automation the user sets up silently does nothing.

title: Bug: Session freezes silently on long multi-step tasks
author: jeankoa
body:
## Description

When Fazm is asked to perform a long multi-step task (e.g., opening 5+ GitHub issues sequentially), the session freezes silently mid-execution. No error is shown, no warning is given — the UI just stops responding as if the task was never started.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Ask Fazm to perform a task requiring multiple sequential tool calls (e.g., "open 5 GitHub issues one by one")
2. Fazm starts the task and confirms it will proceed
3. Session freezes silently — no output, no error, no retry

## Expected Behavior

- Task continues to completion, OR
- Fazm notifies the user of a timeout/failure and offers to retry

## Actual Behavior

- Session hangs silently
- User sees no feedback
- On next message, Fazm starts a new session with no memory of what was happening

## Root Cause (suspected)

- Timeout in the acp-bridge (Node.js) when too many sequential tool calls are chained
- No retry or graceful fallback when the bridge drops the connection

## Impact

Critical — user cannot rely on Fazm to complete long tasks. Work is lost silently.

title: fazm-lock.sh not found
author: rlobrecht
body:
Both run.sh and reset-and-run.sh both reference scripts/fazm-lock.sh, but this file does not exist in the repository. 

Are there alternate instructions to build?

title: Bug: Extended thinking blocks leaking to chat UI (sonnet[1m] / 1M context models)
author: jeankoa
body:
## Bug Description

When using Claude models with extended thinking enabled (e.g. `sonnet[1m]` / 1M context), the internal `thinking` content blocks from the Claude API response are being rendered directly in the chat UI instead of being filtered out.

The user sees raw model reasoning like:
> "achei! ByteVirt HK em 82.27.11.149. vou instalar o MTProxy agora via SSH."

...which is internal chain-of-thought that should never be visible.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Switch model to `sonnet[1m]` (or any Claude model with extended thinking enabled)
2. Ask Fazm to perform a multi-step task
3. Observe that `thinking` block content appears raw in the chat bubbles

## Root Cause

The Claude API returns content blocks with `type: "thinking"` alongside `type: "text"` blocks when extended thinking is enabled. Fazm is not filtering these before rendering the response in the UI.

## Fix

In the response processing layer, filter out content blocks with `type: "thinking"` before displaying to the user. Only render `type: "text"` blocks.

**Example (pseudocode):**
```js
const visibleBlocks = response.content.filter(block => block.type === 'text');
```

Or in Python:
```python
visible_blocks = [b for b in response.content if b.type == "text"]
```

## Workaround (for users now)

Avoid using `sonnet[1m]` — use `claude-sonnet-4-6` (without 1M context) to disable extended thinking until the fix is shipped.

## Impact

- Privacy: user's internal reasoning is exposed
- UX: raw thinking text interrupts the chat flow and confuses the user
- Tasks get abandoned mid-execution when the turn breaks due to the leaked content

## References

- [Anthropic docs — Extended Thinking](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking)

title: Remote control (chat.fazm.ai) not connecting - spins indefinitely on multiple Macs
author: jeankoa
body:
**Bug report submitted on behalf of user Jean Koa**

**Issue:** The remote control feature at chat.fazm.ai fails to connect on 2 different Mac machines. The app appears to be running but the connection never establishes — it just spins indefinitely.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open Fazm on Mac
2. Go to Settings > Remote Control
3. Scan QR code with phone
4. Open chat.fazm.ai on phone
5. Connection spins but never connects

**Troubleshooting already attempted:**
- Regenerated QR code multiple times
- Disabled VPN (Surfshark)
- Confirmed both Macs connected to internet
- Reproduced on 2 different Mac machines — confirms this is a server-side issue

**Expected behavior:** Phone connects and allows remote chat with Fazm

**Actual behavior:** Infinite loading/spinning, no connection established on either Mac

title: Feature Request: Multi-provider support (Ollama local + Grok) + Bug: TTS broken 50h+ - caused by Claude censorship incident
author: jeankoa
body:
Hi Fazm team,

I'm writing to report two active issues and a feature request that are directly related.

## Incident: Claude censorship caused 24h+ of service loss

While using Fazm, I told the agent that its behavior was illegal in my context — specifically, it was refusing to help me connect **my own Telegram bot to my own local system** (2 users: me and my Mac). This is a 100% personal and legal use case.

As a result, Claude censored me and I was **blocked for approximately 24 hours**. During this time:
- My Telegram integration was completely unavailable for 24h
- **TTS has been broken for 50+ hours and still does not work today**

This incident made it clear that depending on a single closed AI provider (Anthropic) creates serious reliability and autonomy risks for users.

## Bug: TTS still broken (50h+)

TTS shows as "disabled" in app settings and does not respond to re-enabling. This has persisted for 50+ hours since the incident.

## Feature Request: Multi-provider backend support

I have an **Apple M5 Max with 128GB RAM** — I can run large local models (35B+ at 8-bit) with no issue. I would like Fazm to support:

1. **Ollama (local models)** — e.g. Qwen3-35B-A3B, fully local, no censorship risk
2. **xAI Grok API** — as an alternative cloud provider
3. **Other OpenAI-compatible APIs** — for flexibility

I noticed you already have `CodexProvider` in the acp-bridge codebase (marked Phase 1, currently unused). This shows you are already thinking about multi-provider support — please prioritize it.

## Why this matters

Users with powerful hardware should not be forced to depend on a single provider that can censor or block legitimate personal use cases. Sovereign AI is a real need, especially for users who have invested in the hardware to run it.

Please let me know if you need logs or additional info.

title: Cannot exit the onboarding flow
author: techieanant
body: The app is stuck in "Setting up Fazm" flow never rendering the "Continue to App" button. Is there an override somewhere?

title: Issue on newest version
author: phillipbruh
body:
-------------------------------------
Translated Report (Full Report Below)
-------------------------------------
Process:             Fazm [3762]
Path:                /Applications/Fazm.app/Contents/MacOS/Fazm
Identifier:          com.fazm.app
Version:             2.0.3 (121)
Code Type:           X86-64 (Native)
Role:                Background
Parent Process:      launchd [1]
Coalition:           com.fazm.app [3307]
User ID:             501

Date/Time:           2026-04-04 22:56:58.0790 -0400
Launch Time:         2026-04-04 22:56:51.7870 -0400
Hardware Model:      MacBookPro16,2
OS Version:          macOS 26.3.1 (25D771280a)
